    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/setup.md
    • Description: Create docs/setup.md detailing how to run locally and via Docker.
    • File Content: markdown "# Getting Started & Setup Guide\n\nThis page explains how to install dependencies, configure environment settings, and run the MCP Explorer locally or inside a Docker Compose environment.\n\n---\n\n## 💻 Method A: Running Locally (Bare Metal)\n\nRunning the application locally is ideal for debugging and editing components without container rebuilding.\n\n### Prerequisites\n1. **Python 3.11+** installed on your system.\n2. **Ollama** installed and running on `localhost:11434`. Pull the default model using:\nbash\n ollama pull gemma4:e4b\n \n\n### 1. Execute Setup Script\nWe provide an interactive `setup.sh` script to automate your local virtual environment instantiation:\nbash\n./setup.sh\n\nThis script will:\n* Create a virtual environment (`venv`).\n* Upgrade `pip` and install project packages declared in `pyproject.toml` (including automated test requirements).\n* Install Playwright browser binaries for e2e automation testing.\n\n### 2. Configure Local Secrets\nCreate a local `.env` file in the project root based on our template:\nbash\ncp .env.example .env\n\nOpen `.env` and fill in your values (e.g. your Langfuse credentials if using observability).\n\n### 3. Run the Chat Application\nActivate the virtual environment and start Chainlit:\nbash\nsource venv/bin/activate\nchainlit run ui/app.py\n\nThe Chat UI will open automatically at: **[http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000)**\n\n---\n\n## 🐳 Method B: Running via Docker Compose Stack\n\nThe Docker Compose setup launches a complete production-ready pipeline, including clickhouse backends, redis caches, minio media uploads, postgres stores, langfuse-server logs visualization, and the chat app.\n\n### 1. Build and Run the Stack\nWe provide a helper launch script `docker-run.sh` that auto-generates your secure encryption keys and starts the build:\nbash\n./docker-run.sh\n\nThis script:\n* Checks for the existence of `.env`.\n* Generates a safe `SALT`, `ENCRYPTION_KEY`, and `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` if not presen <truncated 1121 bytes>

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/configuration.md
    • Description: Create docs/configuration.md detailing env variables and json configs.
    • File Content: markdown "# Configuration Reference Guide\n\nThis document describes all environment variables and configuration files (`toolsConfig.json` and `mcpConfig.json`) used to control the runtime behavior of the MCP Explorer.\n\n---\n\n## 🔑 Environment Variables\n\nThese variables are defined in your local `.env` file (for bare-metal execution) or `.env.docker` (in the Docker Stack).\n\n### Core Services Settings\n\n| Variable Name | Default Value | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `OLLAMA_URL` | `http://localhost:11434` | The HTTP endpoint where your local Ollama server is running. Inside Docker containers, this is overridden to `http://host.docker.internal:11434`. |\n| `MONGODB_URI` | `mongodb://localhost:27017/dev` | Connection URI for the MongoDB server used by the agent memory. |\n| `LOG_LEVEL` | `DEBUG` | System verbosity logging level. Options are `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARNING`, `ERROR`, `CRITICAL`. |\n\n### Langfuse Observatory Connection\n\n| Variable Name | Target UI Location | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` | Settings -> API Credentials | Public API Key generated from the Langfuse project console to authenticate traces. |\n| `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` | Settings -> API Credentials | Secret API Key paired with the public key. Keep this private! |\n| `LANGFUSE_HOST` | - | The HTTP host address of the Langfuse server. For local dev, this is `http://localhost:3000`. Inside Docker, the app container connects via `http://langfuse-server:3000`. |\n\n### Docker Stack Internal Secrets\n\nThese variables are auto-generated when running `./docker-run.sh` to secure your internal Postgres and session cookies:\n\n* `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`: Used by NextAuth to sign secure session tokens in the Langfuse dashboard.\n* `SALT`: Secure string used by Langfuse to encrypt project variables.\n* `ENCRYPTION_KEY`: A 64-character hexadecimal key used by the Langfuse backend to encrypt credential records stored in Postgres.\n\n---\n\n## 🛠️ Configuration Files\n\n### 1. `toolsConfig.json`\nManages standard <truncated 1209 bytes>

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/architecture.md
    • Description: Create docs/architecture.md explaining ReAct loop, MCP, and Langfuse tracing.
    • File Content: markdown "# Technical Architecture & Tracing\n\nThis page explains the internal mechanics of the MCP Explorer agentic system, covering ReAct loops, Model Context Protocol (MCP) transport, and observability integration.\n\n---\n\n## 🔁 The ReAct Agent Loop\nThe agent core utilizes the **ReAct (Reasoning and Acting)** framework to solve tasks iteratively:\n\n1. **Reasoning**: The model (e.g. `gemma4:e4b`) analyzes the user's prompt and decides which tool (if any) to call, along with the required parameters.\n2. **Acting**: The agent framework intercepts this decision and executes the tool locally or via MCP.\n3. **Observation**: The tool output is appended to the message history, and the model evaluates if the answer is complete or if further actions are required.\n\nThis cycle continues dynamically until the model provides a final output.\n\n\n ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n │ Human Input Message │\n └──────────────┬──────────────┘\n │\n ▼\n ┌───────────────────────────┐\n ┌───► │ LLM Reasoning Step │\n │ └─────────────┬─────────────┘\n │ │\n │ [Decision]\n │ / \\n │ [Tool Call] [Final Answer] ──► [Respond to User]\n │ │\n │ ▼\n │ ┌───────────┐\n │ │ Tool Exec │\n │ └─────┬─────┘\n │ │\n │ [Observation]\n └───────────┘\n\n\n---\n\n## 🔌 Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration\nThe Model Context Protocol allows the agent to communicate with separate external tools running as independent subprocesses.\n\n- **Process Isolation**: MCP s <truncated 1676 bytes>

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I will update the task.md to reflect that docs/architecture.md has been created.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: replace_file_content ✏️

    I will create docs/components.md to detail the source files and components of the codebase, explaining their purpose and implementation.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/components.md
    • Description: Create docs/components.md explaining each Python source file.
    • File Content: markdown "# Codebase Component Breakdown\n\nThis document provides a breakdown of each Python file in the MCP Explorer codebase, explaining its significance and architectural role.\n\n---\n\n## 🛠️ Configuration & Core Files\n\n### 1. `config.py`\nThe central configuration hub of the application. It manages dynamic configuration reloading based on environments.\n* **Significance**: Loads variables from `.env`, `.env.test`, or `.env.prod` depending on the value of the `MCP_ENV` environment variable (falling back to `.env` if undefined).\n* **Key Objects**: `CONFIG` (dictionary housing `OLLAMA_URL`, `MONGODB_URI`, `LOG_LEVEL`, and pathways to tools/MCP configuration files).\n\n### 2. `core/agent.py`\nThe brain of the system.\n* **Significance**: Defines the custom `Agent` class wrapper. It instantiates `ChatOllama` utilizing the `gemma4:e4b` model and binds it to a list of dynamically resolved tools using LangGraph's `create_react_agent`.\n* **Key Methods**:\n * `ainvoke(prompt, thread_id, callbacks)`: Starts the agent as an asynchronous generator utilizing `astream_events` to yield granular execution events (model responses, token outputs, tool inputs/outputs) back to the caller.\n\n### 3. `core/logger.py`\nThe diagnostic tracing console callback.\n* **Significance**: Extends LangChain's `BaseCallbackHandler` to format and print colored, structured execution logs directly to the system CLI.\n* **Key Methods**:\n * `on_chain_start()` / `on_chain_end()`: Logs execution boundaries of sub-graphs.\n * `on_tool_start()` / `on_tool_end()`: Traces the exact input and outputs of standard/MCP tool execution.\n\n---\n\n## 🔌 Loader Components\n\n### 4. `core/mcp_loader.py`\nConnects the agent to external tool suites using the Model Context Protocol.\n* **Significance**: Parses `mcpConfig.json`, spawns each configured MCP server (e.g. SQLite, local scripts) as an independent OS process, establishes stdin/stdout channels, and maps their exposed JSON-RPC tools into LangChain compatible tool interfaces. <truncated 1133 bytes>

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/testing.md
    • Description: Create docs/testing.md detailing unit testing and manual testing scripts.
    • File Content: markdown "# Testing & Verification Guide\n\nThis page explains how to run automated unit and integration tests, as well as execute manual verification scripts to check system integrity, agent loops, and observability tracing.\n\n---\n\n## 🧪 1. Automated Testing (Pytest)\n\nWe use `pytest` (with `pytest-asyncio`) to run isolated configuration, loader, and integration tests.\n\n### Running the Tests\nTo run the automated test suite, activate your virtual environment and execute `pytest`, ignoring the manual tests folder:\nbash\nsource venv/bin/activate\npytest --ignore=tests/manual\n\n\n### Automated Test Suite Descriptions\n* **`tests/test_config.py`**: Verifies that environment variable profiles (`default`, `test`, `prod`) reload variables (like `OLLAMA_URL` and `MONGODB_URI`) correctly from their respective `.env` files.\n* **`tests/test_tool_loader.py`**: Validates the standard tools dynamic dynamic initializer, verifying the execution of Python code definitions inside `toolsConfig.json`.\n* **`tests/test_integration.py`**: Asserts successful connectivity with a running Ollama container and verifies that the `Agent` is created with the expected endpoints.\n\n---\n\n## 🛠️ 2. Manual Testing Scripts (`tests/manual/`)\n\nManual test scripts are divided into language folders to separate browser automation tests from diagnostic script tests.\n\n### A. Python Verification Scripts (`tests/manual/python/`)\n\nThese scripts test specific API elements or simulate trace outputs directly inside the agent environment.\n\n* **`test_logging.py`**: Simulates two separate agent executions (one successful, one catching a tool division-by-zero boundary error) to verify that trace payloads and console logs are formatted correctly.\n* **`generate_trace.py`**: Runs a simple query through the ReAct agent, flushes the Langfuse callback client, and confirms trace logs.\n* **`generate_trace_simple.py`**: Directly invokes the Langfuse SDK to create a raw trace and span, verifying authorization keys.\n* **`verify_api.py` <truncated 1124 bytes>

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/docs/examples.md
    • Description: Create docs/examples.md detailing prompts and configs.
    • File Content: markdown "# Code & Prompts Examples\n\nThis page provides practical examples of prompts, JSON configurations, and procedures for extending the capabilities of your MCP Explorer.\n\n---\n\n## 💬 1. Example User Prompts\n\nYou can enter these queries into the Chainlit chat input to test the system's reasoning loop:\n\n### Standard Tools Queries\n* *Prompt*: `use wikipedia and search for Rabindranath Tagore`\n * **Agent Flow**: Decides to execute the `wikipedia` tool, scrapes the summary of Tagore, and uses the text to answer.\n* *Prompt*: `use arxiv and search for papers on Transformer architectures`\n * **Agent Flow**: Decides to run the `arxiv` tool, fetches recent publications matching the query, and formats the abstracts in Markdown.\n\n### Combined Tool Queries\n* *Prompt*: `Search wikipedia for the formula of water, then search arxiv for recent papers detailing water purification techniques using that formula.`\n * **Agent Flow**: Executes a sequential tool-calling chain (first Wikipedia, then uses the extracted data to formulate an Arxiv search query).\n\n---\n\n## 🔌 2. Adding a Custom MCP Server\n\nYou can extend the agent's capabilities by adding server definitions to `mcpConfig.json`.\n\n### Example: SQLite MCP Server\nTo allow the agent to read, write, and query a local database:\n\n1. Open `mcpConfig.json` in your workspace.\n2. Add the SQLite server profile to the `mcpServers` object:\njson\n {\n \"mcpServers\": {\n \"sqlite\": {\n \"command\": \"uvx\",\n \"args\": [\n \"mcp-server-sqlite\",\n \"--db-path\",\n \"/Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/dev.db\"\n ]\n }\n }\n }\n \n3. Restart your Chainlit server or rebuild the Docker container.\n4. Ask the agent: `Show the tables in the SQLite database and list their schemas.` The agent will call the SQLite server tools dynamically.\n\n---\n\n## 🛠️ 3. Adding a Dynamic Tool Initialization\n\nIf a new tool requires specific conf <truncated 554 bytes>

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I have successfully set up the complete developer documentation site under the /docs folder using MkDocs (configured with the premium material theme), ensuring it is fully compatible with local serving and publication to GitHub Pages. All documentation references to the client project have been sanitized, replacing "MCP" with "MCP".

    Here is a summary of the files created and configured:

    1. MkDocs Configuration & Ignore Files

    • mkdocs.yml: Created in the root directory to define site metadata, color themes (light/dark adaptive mode), pymdown extensions for formatting, and page navigation structure.
    • .gitignore: Updated to ignore local compiled HTML site outputs (site/) to prevent repository bloating.

    2. Documentation Pages (under /docs)

    • index.md (Home / Overview): Features, benefits, and flow diagram of the MCP Explorer.
    • setup.md (Getting Started): Interactive step-by-step setup guides for both local execution and the Docker Compose stack.
    • configuration.md (Configuration Reference): Explains each environment variable and JSON config structure (mcpConfig.json, toolsConfig.json).
    • architecture.md (Architecture): Breakdown of the ReAct loops, JSON-RPC stdout/stdin communication of MCP servers, and Langfuse tracing.
    • components.md (Codebase Components): Descriptions of the functions and significance of all python source files (core/agent.py, core/mcp_loader.py, etc.).
    • testing.md (Testing Reference): Guide for automated testing (pytest) and running manual browser tests (Puppeteer/Playwright scripts).
    • examples.md (Code Examples): Prompting guides, dynamic tool inits, and steps for adding a custom SQLite MCP server.
    • walkthrough.md (Walkthrough): Sanitized implementation walkthrough explaining historical milestones.

    3. Log Parsing & Conversational Log

    • parse_logs.py: Created a Python script that reads the raw transcript.jsonl development log, extracts requests and responses, sanitizes the naming, and automatically formats them into a clean Markdown timeline.
    • session_history.md: Generated using the script, compiling the complete conversational history.

    4. Verification & Commit

    • Verified that the MkDocs site compiles cleanly without warnings or errors.
    • Staged all files and created a new Git commit. The repository's working tree is clean.

    👤 User Request (2026-06-05 14:01:30Z)

    how to run mkdocs?

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    To run the documentation site locally or build it for deployment, follow these steps:

    1. Install MkDocs

    Activate your virtual environment and install mkdocs along with the material design theme: 

    # Activate your local python virtual environment
source venv/bin/activate

# Install MkDocs and the Material theme
pip install mkdocs-material

    2. Run the Development Server (Local Preview)

    To preview the documentation site locally with live-reloading (automatically updates when you save markdown files): 

    mkdocs serve
    Once started, open your browser and navigate to: 👉 http://localhost:8000

    (If port 8000 is already in use by the Chainlit application, MkDocs will automatically use another port like 8001, or you can specify one manually: mkdocs serve -a localhost:8002)

    3. Build the Static HTML (For GitHub Pages/Hosting)

    To generate the static HTML and asset files for hosting (e.g. uploading to GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel): 

    mkdocs build
    This will compile all Markdown files under /docs and output them to a new /site folder in the project root. (Note: /site is ignored in Git to keep the repository clean).

1: # MCP Explorer 🚀
2: 
3: MCP Explorer is a local-first, multi-agent query and search system. It leverages LangChain and LangGraph for orchestration, Chainlit for a responsive chat UI, Ollama for local LLM inference (`gemma4:e4b`), and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to load dynamic capabilities.
4: 
5: Additionally, the system features out-of-the-box observability with Langfuse and console trace logging.
6: 
7: ---
8:

13:                         │ Chainlit UI

    I have successfully completed the directory restructuring and build configuration for GitHub Pages deployment:

    1. Renamed /docs to /docs-md: Moved the Markdown source documentation files to the new docs-md directory.
    2. Updated Configurations:
    3. Configured docs_dir: docs-md inside mkdocs.yml to specify docs-md as the Markdown source directory.
    4. Configured site_dir: docs inside mkdocs.yml to set the output target directory to docs.
    5. Updated the logging parser script tests/manual/python/parse_logs.py to write session_history.md to the new docs-md path.
    6. Updated the directory tree layout in README.md.
    7. Re-ran Log Parser & MkDocs Compile:
    8. Re-executed the log parser to write the conversational log history to docs-md/session_history.md.
    9. Built the site with mkdocs build inside a temporary environment. It automatically compiled from docs-md directly into the docs folder.
    10. Git Commit:
    11. Staged the files and committed the changes. The Git workspace is completely clean.

    The compiled static HTML files now reside inside /docs in the project root, making it fully ready to be published to GitHub Pages, while developers can still run it locally and modify the Markdown source in /docs-md.

    👤 User Request (2026-06-05 14:05:55Z)

    Is now /docs folder compatible to be hosted in github pages and works like anyother website?

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    Yes, the /docs folder is now fully compatible and optimized for hosting on GitHub Pages! It will function as a fully static, responsive website.

    Here is why it works and how to enable it:

    1. Why it is Compatible

    • Entry Point: MkDocs compiled your home page directly to docs/index.html. GitHub Pages automatically looks for an index.html file in the folder you target to serve as the website's homepage.
    • Relative Asset Routing: MkDocs is designed to write relative link paths (e.g., assets/stylesheets/..., setup/index.html) rather than absolute ones. This means all CSS stylesheets, JavaScript files, search indices, and pages will resolve correctly, whether the site is hosted on a sub-path (like https://your-username.github.io/NKExplorer/) or a custom domain.

    2. How to Enable GitHub Pages for Your Repository

    Once you push these committed changes to your remote GitHub repository, follow these quick steps:

    1. Go to your repository page on GitHub.
    2. Click on the Settings tab (gear icon) in the top menu.
    3. Scroll down to the Code and automation section in the left sidebar and click on Pages.
    4. Under the Build and deployment section:
      • Set Source to Deploy from a branch.
      • Under Branch, select your default branch (e.g., master or main).
      • In the folder dropdown next to the branch, change / (root) to /docs.
    5. Click Save.

    GitHub will start a background build action. Within a couple of minutes, your site will be live at: 👉 https://<your-github-username>.github.io/<your-repo-name>/

    👤 User Request (2026-06-05 14:07:27Z)

    when i access configuration, there is one more index.html, from local, it is showing the configuration and index.html as a list element instead of rendering, I have to click on it explicitly

    🤖 Assistant Thought & Response

    I will add use_directory_urls: false to mkdocs.yml. This tells MkDocs to compile pages into flat .html files (e.g., configuration.html instead of configuration/index.html). This fixes navigation when browsing files directly via the browser's file:// protocol (double-clicking the files locally) without running a web server.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: replace_file_content ✏️

    • Target Content: yaml docs_dir: docs-md site_dir: docs
    • Replacement Content: yaml docs_dir: docs-md site_dir: docs use_directory_urls: false

    I will modify /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/tests/manual/python/parse_logs.py to add a sophisticated formatter for tool calls. Instead of outputting raw, doubly-escaped JSON text, it will parse tool arguments and format code contents (like files written or bash commands) in their own clean, unescaped, language-highlighted Markdown code blocks.

    🛠️ Tool Executions

    • Tool: write_to_file 📄
    • Target File: /Users/kathi.s/NKExplorer/MCP_Explorer/tests/manual/python/parse_logs.py
    • Description: Modify parse_logs.py to format JSON arguments and code strings inside tool executions into clean Markdown code blocks.
    • File Content: python "import json\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ndef sanitize(text):\n if not text:\n return \"\"\n # Case insensitive replacement of MCP with MCP\n text = re.sub(r'(?i)MCP', 'MCP', text)\n return text\n\ndef get_language(filename):\n if not filename:\n return \"\"\n filename = filename.lower()\n if filename.endswith(\".py\"):\n return \"python\"\n if filename.endswith(\".yml\") or filename.endswith(\".yaml\"):\n return \"yaml\"\n if filename.endswith(\".json\"):\n return \"json\"\n if filename.endswith(\".sh\"):\n return \"bash\"\n if filename.endswith(\".md\"):\n return \"markdown\"\n if filename.endswith(\".toml\"):\n return \"toml\"\n if filename.endswith(\".js\"):\n return \"javascript\"\n if filename.endswith(\".html\"):\n return \"html\"\n return \"\"\n\ndef format_tool_call(name, args):\n if isinstance(args, str):\n try:\n args = json.loads(args)\n except:\n pass\n \n if not isinstance(args, dict):\n return f\"- **Tool**: `{name}`\\ntext\n{args}\n \\n\"\n\n lines = []\n \n if name == \"write_to_file\":\n target = args.get(\"TargetFile\", \"\").strip('\"')\n desc = args.get(\"Description\", \"\").strip('\"')\n content = args.get(\"CodeContent\", \"\")\n \n if isinstance(content, str):\n if content.startswith('\"') and content.endswith('\"'):\n try:\n content = json.loads(content)\n except:\n content = content[1:-1]\n \n lines.append(f\"- **Tool**: `write_to_file` 📄\\n\")\n if target:\n lines.append(f\" - **Target File**: `{target}`\\n\")\n if desc:\n lines.append(f\" - **Description**: {desc}\\n\")\n if content:\n lang = get_language(target)\n lines.append(f\" - **File Content**:\\n\")\n lines.append(f\ <truncated 7615 bytes>
